title: Jerg
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image= |name=Jerg |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|The people on board this ship owe you their lives"
"Don't owe me nothing but money, Morgan Katarn. Nothing but money.|[[Morgan Katarn]] and Jerg, before arriving to [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]]|}} '''Jerg''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] who lived during the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was commanding the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] called the ''[[Cyclops]]''. He chanced upon the forgotten planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] which he made his base of operation, unaware of its rich ancient history. He was responsible for relocating several [[Sulon/Legends|Sulonese]] there.''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' ==Biography== Around [[11 BBY/Legends|11 BBY]], he made a blind jump through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] to escape an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], and as a result rediscovered [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], a planet that was not in any contemporary [[Star map/Legends|Star-charts]]. His crew used a location on Ruusan as his base of operations, called [[Fort Nowhere]], to store contraband. ===Relocating the Sulonese=== Ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, [[Morgan Katarn]] hired him and his ship to replant three hundred fourty-seven [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]]-sympathiser colonists from [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] onto Ruusan, whom he collected from a [[Unidentified space station (Sullust system)|space station]] they were hiding. After a long and uneasy trip, two [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] before arrival, he summoned Katarn to notify that they would be ready for disembarking.[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] Standing next to Katarn looking over Ruusan through the [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]], Jerg ensured him that it was a planet of human-normal [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] and [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and the fact that the Empire still hadn't discovered his operations there made it safe for them; but, displaying some honesty, he couldn't ensure him that an Imperial [[Probe droid/Legends|probe]] would not discover them sometime. He also mentioned the existence of ruins and an [[Bouncer|indigenous species]] not worth considering. Katarn thanked him, saying that his people owe him their lives, but Jerg dismissed his thanking, as he was doing a job. After the Sulonese landed on the planet on [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]], Jerg showed Fort Nowhere to Katarn; Katarn asked whether the planet was indeed empty, and Jerg admitted that one of his pilots had noticed "[[Valley of the Jedi|something]]" in the badlands while bringing the people there. Jerg refused Katarn's request to send a recon team, as it wasn't in their agreement, considering also that the coordinates were in the badlands. However he did lent to Katarn his [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]], the ''[[Old Codger]]''. When Katarn was a hundred [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] west from the landing site, Jerg contacted him via [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] to ask if he knew what he was doing, and reminded him to give the speeder a cool-down; Katarn reported that he was following the remnants of a highway. Later Katarn called him to report that he camped by a huge rock, which seemed artificial to him; Jerg was momentarily worried when he heard Katarn stumpled into a hole; Katarn mentioned several even-sized and even-spaced holes like it around, then he reported movements and strange feelings. Jerg insisted on learning the facts. The moment he cautioned Katarn to play it safe, the comlink went dead, and Jerg thought it was either an interference by the rock formation, or cut on purpose by something. The next day Katarn contacted him again, saying that he spent the night where they last talked, moving away from the point of interference, and would like to scout around a little more before returning; Morgan avoided mentioning that his last location was an [[Olmondo|ancient city]] where he had made contact with the native species, and was heading towards [[Valley of the Jedi|another]]. Jerg told him to report anything interesting he would find. Not long after, Morgan returned from his trip and rejoined with Jerg, but he avoided mentioning anything about his discoveries. ===Later years=== Jerg continued to operate after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|fall]] of the Empire. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] he visited Ruusan again, before going offworld; thirty days after his departure, the [[Battle of Fort Nowhere]] began. ==Personality and traits== Jerg was tall and gaunt. He wore old dirty clothes, a sweaty tank top, and pants that had lost their white color. To show his rank among his crew, he wore a [[Captain/Legends|Captain's]] [[Hat/Legends|cap]] from the time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Other than that, he preferred to walk around barefoot. Despite his unkempt appearance, and his inventory being consisted of old hardware, he was careful to keep everything in good repair, like the ''[[Old Codger]]''. Despite their good cooperation, Morgan knew that Jerg was above all a mercenary, eager to make quick profit from anything he could put his hand on; knowing that the Bouncers preferred the Valley to be kept a secret, and had to lie to Jerg about not finding anything interesting. ==Behind the scenes== In the audio adaptation of [[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'']], he was voiced by [[Tom Keith]]. When [[Morgan Katarn]] refers to him, his name can be misheard for "[[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]," the antagonist of the story. ==Appearances== *''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' {{1stm}} *[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] *''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Smugglers]]
